preamble: The voiced retroflex lateral approximant [ɭ] shifts to the palatal lateral approximant [ʎ] through fronting and deretroflexion in South Asian languages. This transformation reflects place-of-articulation changes in lateral consonant systems, documented as allophonic variation in Dravidian and Indo-Aryan languages.

examples.note: Allophonic variation in certain phonological contexts
Casual speech variant of retroflex lateral
